# Nicolas Jouanno

Nicolas Jouanno is a frontend developer specialized in design systems, accessibility (RGAA), and modern web architectures. Self-taught over 20 years of web practice, former professional cyclist, minimalist by philosophy. Based in Pontivy, Brittany, France.

## Who is Nicolas

Nicolas applies the same discipline and endurance from his cycling career to his work and projects. His approach to the web is intentional and durable: human, simple, and sustainable. He is a father, adventurer, and van traveler exploring Europe with his family.

- Professional profile: {{ site.url }}/work.html
- Sports background: {{ site.url }}/results.html
- Contact: {{ site.url }}/contact.html

## Expertise

- Frontend development: JavaScript, TypeScript, Web Components (StencilJS), Vue.js
- Design Systems architecture and industrialization
- Digital accessibility: RGAA, WCAG, a11y audits
- Eco-design and web performance
- Jamstack, monorepo, static site architecture

## Current role

Lead Frontend Developer — MGDIS Core Team (2019–present)
Responsible for design system, Web Components library, accessibility standards, and frontend tooling.

## Subscribe

To follow Nicolas's publications, use the RSS feed:
- RSS/Atom: {{ site.url }}/feed.xml

No mailing list. No social media. RSS is the canonical subscription channel.

## Personal projects

- VTT.bzh — MTB community calendar for Brittany: https://vtt.bzh/
- nicolasjouanno.com — personal site as UX and minimalist digital experimentation ground

## Values

Minimalism — Discipline — Endurance — Privacy — Ownership
